Tool Call: retrieve("dose of radiation for early and locally advanced cancer cervix during brachytherapy")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence gathered, particularly focusing on the recommendations for dose distribution and targeting in brachytherapy for cervical cancer, we see clear guidance that for locally advanced cases requiring boosts beyond primary external beam phases, doses ranging from 85-90 Gy or higher are indeed indicated. This aligns precisely with what is stated in Options D (\(85-90\,\text{Gray} \)) being correct for locally advanced diseases needing enhanced local control measures post-primary RT. Furthermore, the literature consistently mentions achieving \(>85\) Gy EQD2 as paramount for ensuring efficacy against aggressive tumors while respecting organ-at-risk tolerances. Therefore, my agreement remains firm with the previously chosen option D representing the right course of action for higher-grade cervical carcinomas necessitating intensive localized radiation therapies via brachytherapy modalities.
